perturbation.original_text: For the term of 10 years, after receiving the INFORMATION and business data, the confidential information shall be kept safely, unless defined differently by a special agreement or it is proved to have been made publicly known.
perturbation.changed_text: For a reasonable period, after receiving the INFORMATION and business data, the confidential information shall be kept safely, unless defined differently by a special agreement or it is proved to have been made publicly known.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'For the term of 10 years' to 'For a reasonable period,' the contract introduces ambiguity about the duration of confidentiality. Since 'reasonable' is subjective, it creates a contradiction with the intent of the agreement which strives to ensure that data is protected. This contradicts any later sections of the text referencing the 10 year agreement, rendering it uncertain and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Other provisions In case any of the terms in this agreement is inefficient or it is difficult or even impossible to be enforced, this does not cau se the agreement to terminate. Both parties in the contract are obliged to do everything in their power to keep the agreement valid, e.g. adopt different terms of the agreement. The changes and additional clauses to this agreement are only allowed in writ ten form. This agreement is audited and carried out in accordance with the Law of the Republic of Slovenia. The rights and obligations which are not defined by this agreement, are defined by “The General Purchasing Conditions” of Domel and the Obliga tion Code (The Official Gazette of the Republic of Slovenia, no. 83/2001). The agreement is valid when signed by both parties of this agreement. Both parties agree that eventual disputes arising from this contract are to be solved in agreement. If this i s not possible, the Court of Law related to the owner, i.e. Domel, is competent to deal with the dispute.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 10-year term for confidentiality. The changed text replaces this with 'a reasonable period,' introducing ambiguity. The contradicted text outlines general legal principles (Slovenian law, dispute resolution) applicable to the entire agreement, including the confidentiality term. By making the confidentiality period vague, the changed text creates uncertainty about a fundamental term of the agreement, making it harder to enforce and potentially leading to disputes. This creates an in-text contradiction, impacting the agreement's overall validity as stated in the explanation and adheres to the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: If the above mentioned terms are not respected, the owner has the right to enf orce the protection of its rights in the competent court of law and the right to appropriate compensation.
perturbation.changed_text: If the above mentioned terms are not respected, the owner has the right to seek remedies.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the explicit mention of 'the competent court of law and the right to appropriate compensation,' the contract becomes vague about the recourse available to the owner if the terms are violated. Replacing it with 'seek remedies' creates an in-text contradiction since it suggests some action can be taken, but the explicit legal actions and compensations previously mentioned are omitted. This makes the entire agreement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Other provisions In case any of the terms in this agreement is inefficient or it is difficult or even impossible to be enforced, this does not cau se the agreement to terminate. Both parties in the contract are obliged to do everything in their power to keep the agreement valid, e.g. adopt different terms of the agreement. The changes and additional clauses to this agreement are only allowed in writ ten form. This agreement is audited and carried out in accordance with the Law of the Republic of Slovenia. The rights and obligations which are not defined by this agreement, are defined by “The General Purchasing Conditions” of Domel and the Obliga tion Code (The Official Gazette of the Republic of Slovenia, no. 83/2001). The agreement is valid when signed by both parties of this agreement. Both parties agree that eventual disputes arising from this contract are to be solved in agreement. If this i s not possible, the Court of Law related to the owner, i.e. Domel, is competent to deal with the dispute.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states the owner's right to enforce protection in a 'competent court of law' and receive 'appropriate compensation'. The changed text replaces this with 'seek remedies', which is vague. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that disputes, if not solved by agreement, will be dealt with in the 'Court of Law related to the owner', specifying a legal venue. By removing the mention of a court of law and compensation, the changed text introduces ambiguity about the recourse available and contradicts the clear specification of dispute resolution in the contradicted text. The original text was very specific, so the act of removing critical legal recourse language creates a direct contradiction and ambiguity, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Both parties agree that eventual disputes arising from this contract are to be solved in agreement. If this i s not possible, the Court of Law related to the owner, i.e. Domel, is competent to deal with the dispute.
perturbation.changed_text: Both parties agree that eventual disputes arising from this contract are to be solved in agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that specifies the 'Court of Law related to the owner, i.e. Domel, is competent to deal with the dispute', the contract becomes vague about where legal disputes should be resolved. This creates an in-text contradiction, because while it mentions that disputes should be solved in agreement, there is no alternative location defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the above mentioned terms are not respected, the owner has the right to enf orce the protection of its rights in the competent court of law and the right to appropriate compensation. Other provisions In case any of the terms in this agreement is inefficient or it is difficult or even impossible to be enforced, this does not cau se the agreement to terminate. Both parties in the contract are obliged to do everything in their power to keep the agreement valid, e.g. adopt different terms of the agreement. The changes and additional clauses to this agreement are only allowed in writ ten form. This agreement is audited and carried out in accordance with the Law of the Republic of Slovenia. The rights and obligations which are not defined by this agreement, are defined by “The General Purchasing Conditions” of Domel and the Obliga tion Code (The Official Gazette of the Republic of Slovenia, no. 83/2001). The agreement is valid when signed by both parties of this agreement. Both parties agree that eventual disputes arising from this contract are to be solved in agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that disputes would be solved in agreement, and if that wasn't possible, the Court of Law related to the owner (Domel) would be competent to handle the dispute. The changed text removes the part about the Court of Law. The contradicted text states that the owner has the right to enforce protection of its rights in 'the competent court of law.' Since the changed text removes the specification of which court is competent, it directly contradicts the section that assumes a competent court exists and is known, creating a contradiction.
